topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with the weight of a stone. Subject line: Academic Integrity Violation. My hands, gripping the mouse, began to tremble so violently I had to stop typing entirely. I reread the accusation, the specific passages cited, and a strange, cold emptiness spread through my chest. It felt like the floor had dropped out from under me. I slowly pushed my chair back, the scrape of the wheels against the linoleum sounding deafening in the quiet room. My breath hitched, shallow and quick. I looked around the dorm room, at the posters and the scattered textbooks, and couldn't focus on any of it. A dizzying sensation washed over me, making the fluorescent light above seem to hum with unbearable intensity.

[story 2]

When Professor Albright called my name in the lecture hall, the sound didn't register as mere auditory input; it was a physical blow. My vision tunneled for a moment, the vibrant colors of the lecture slides blurring into an indistinct smear. I felt a sudden, intense pressure behind my eyes, a tightness in my throat that made swallowing feel like sandpaper. When the accusation was finally voiced—a quiet, measured tone that somehow amplified the gravity of the situation—my mind went completely blank. I could only stare at the professor, my mouth dry and refusing to form a coherent response. My legs felt strangely heavy, rooted to the spot as if gravity itself had increased tenfold.

[story 3]

I found the printed notification tucked beneath my sociology textbook. The paper felt brittle, almost accusatory under my fingertips. As I read the words, the room seemed to tilt. A wave of nausea rolled over me, making my stomach clench painfully. I dropped the paper onto my desk, and the sound of the slight impact was startlingly loud. I stood up too quickly, knocking my chair slightly askew, and for a moment, I couldn't process the sequence of events. My gaze kept flickering between the text and the empty space on the wall, my entire body rigid with an unfamiliar, profound stillness. Every nerve ending felt hyper-alert, buzzing with an overwhelming sense of disorientation.
